page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6r.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the arm64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs using the Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor, lacks local NVMe SSD storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14296.73 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6111.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 568.34 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 86.12 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core (1095.0) and Passmark CPU encryption (1303.69) scoring in the lower tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 Ampere Altra architecture offers a resource-dense profile for multi-threaded workloads without the premium of traditional x86 architectures. Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core capabilities, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or heavy cryptographic processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6r.2xlarge is an arm64-based general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this KVM-based server lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6111.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 14296.73, though single-core performance and cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff. The instance offers a cost-efficient hardware profile for multi-threaded, resource-dense workloads. It is suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
